---
name: diagnose
description: Disciplined diagnosis loop for hard bugs and performance regressions. Reproduce → minimise → hypothesise → instrument → fix → regression-test. Use when user says "diagnose this" / "debug this", reports a bug, says something is broken/throwing/failing, or describes a performance regression.
---

# Diagnose

A discipline for hard bugs. Skip phases only when explicitly justified.

When exploring the codebase, use the project's domain glossary to get a clear mental model of the relevant modules, and check ADRs in the area you're touching.

## Phase 1 — Build a feedback loop

**This is the skill.** Everything else is mechanical. If you have a fast, deterministic, agent-runnable pass/fail signal for the bug, you will find the cause — bisection, hypothesis-testing, and instrumentation all just consume that signal. If you don't have one, no amount of staring at code will save you.

Spend disproportionate effort here. **Be aggressive. Be creative. Refuse to give up.**

### Ways to construct one — try them in roughly this order

1. **Failing test** at whatever seam reaches the bug — unit, integration, e2e.
2. **Curl / HTTP script** against a running dev server.
3. **CLI invocation** with a fixture input, diffing stdout against a known-good snapshot.
4. **Headless browser script** (Playwright / Puppeteer) — drives the UI, asserts on DOM/console/network.
5. **Replay a captured trace.** Save a real network request / payload / event log to disk; replay it through the code path in isolation.
6. **Throwaway harness.** Spin up a minimal subset of the system (one service, mocked deps) that exercises the bug code path with a single function call.
7. **Property / fuzz loop.** If the bug is "sometimes wrong output", run 1000 random inputs and look for the failure mode.
8. **Bisection harness.** If the bug appeared between two known states (commit, dataset, version), automate "boot at state X, check, repeat" so you can `git bisect run` it.
9. **Differential loop.** Run the same input through old-version vs new-version (or two configs) and diff outputs.
10. **HITL bash script.** Last resort. If a human must click, drive _them_ with `scripts/hitl-loop.template.sh` so the loop is still structured. Captured output feeds back to you.

Build the right feedback loop, and the bug is 90% fixed.

### Iterate on the loop itself

Treat the loop as a product. Once you have _a_ loop, ask:

- Can I make it faster? (Cache setup, skip unrelated init, narrow the test scope.)
- Can I make the signal sharper? (Assert on the specific symptom, not "didn't crash".)
- Can I make it more deterministic? (Pin time, seed RNG, isolate filesystem, freeze network.)

A 30-second flaky loop is barely better than no loop. A 2-second deterministic loop is a debugging superpower.

### Non-deterministic bugs

The goal is not a clean repro but a **higher reproduction rate**. Loop the trigger 100×, parallelise, add stress, narrow timing windows, inject sleeps. A 50%-flake bug is debuggable; 1% is not — keep raising the rate until it's debuggable.

### When you genuinely cannot build a loop

Stop and say so explicitly. List what you tried. Ask the user for: (a) access to whatever environment reproduces it, (b) a captured artifact (HAR file, log dump, core dump, screen recording with timestamps), or (c) permission to add temporary production instrumentation. Do **not** proceed to hypothesise without a loop.

Do not proceed to Phase 2 until you have a loop you believe in.

## Phase 2 — Reproduce

Run the loop. Watch the bug appear.

Confirm:

- [ ] The loop produces the failure mode the **user** described — not a different failure that happens to be nearby. Wrong bug = wrong fix.
- [ ] The failure is reproducible across multiple runs (or, for non-deterministic bugs, reproducible at a high enough rate to debug against).
- [ ] You have captured the exact symptom (error message, wrong output, slow timing) so later phases can verify the fix actually addresses it.

Do not proceed until you reproduce the bug.

## Phase 3 — Hypothesise

Generate **3–5 ranked hypotheses** before testing any of them. Single-hypothesis generation anchors on the first plausible idea.

Each hypothesis must be **falsifiable**: state the prediction it makes.

> Format: "If <X> is the cause, then <changing Y> will make the bug disappear / <changing Z> will make it worse."

If you cannot state the prediction, the hypothesis is a vibe — discard or sharpen it.

**Show the ranked list to the user before testing.** They often have domain knowledge that re-ranks instantly ("we just deployed a change to #3"), or know hypotheses they've already ruled out. Cheap checkpoint, big time saver. Don't block on it — proceed with your ranking if the user is AFK.

## Phase 4 — Instrument

Each probe must map to a specific prediction from Phase 3. **Change one variable at a time.**

Tool preference:

1. **Debugger / REPL inspection** if the env supports it. One breakpoint beats ten logs.
2. **Targeted logs** at the boundaries that distinguish hypotheses.
3. Never "log everything and grep".

**Tag every debug log** with a unique prefix, e.g. `[DEBUG-a4f2]`. Cleanup at the end becomes a single grep. Untagged logs survive; tagged logs die.

**Perf branch.** For performance regressions, logs are usually wrong. Instead: establish a baseline measurement (timing harness, `performance.now()`, profiler, query plan), then bisect. Measure first, fix second.

## Phase 5 — Fix + regression test

Write the regression test **before the fix** — but only if there is a **correct seam** for it.

A correct seam is one where the test exercises the **real bug pattern** as it occurs at the call site. If the only available seam is too shallow (single-caller test when the bug needs multiple callers, unit test that can't replicate the chain that triggered the bug), a regression test there gives false confidence.

**If no correct seam exists, that itself is the finding.** Note it. The codebase architecture is preventing the bug from being locked down. Flag this for the next phase.

If a correct seam exists:

1. Turn the minimised repro into a failing test at that seam.
2. Watch it fail.
3. Apply the fix.
4. Watch it pass.
5. Re-run the Phase 1 feedback loop against the original (un-minimised) scenario.

## Phase 6 — Cleanup + post-mortem

Required before declaring done:

- [ ] Original repro no longer reproduces (re-run the Phase 1 loop)
- [ ] Regression test passes (or absence of seam is documented)
- [ ] All `[DEBUG-...]` instrumentation removed (`grep` the prefix)
- [ ] Throwaway prototypes deleted (or moved to a clearly-marked debug location)
- [ ] The hypothesis that turned out correct is stated in the commit / PR message — so the next debugger learns

**Then ask: what would have prevented this bug?** If the answer involves architectural change (no good test seam, tangled callers, hidden coupling) hand off to the `/improve-codebase-architecture` skill with the specifics. Make the recommendation **after** the fix is in, not before — you have more information now than when you started.

RESULT 2

name: karpathy-guidelines
description: Behavioral guidelines to reduce common LLM coding mistakes. Use when writing, reviewing, or refactoring code to avoid overcomplication, make surgical changes, surface assumptions, and define verifiable success criteria.
license: MIT
---

# Karpathy Guidelines

Behavioral guidelines to reduce common LLM coding mistakes, derived from [Andrej Karpathy's observations](https://x.com/karpathy/status/2015883857489522876) on LLM coding pitfalls.

**Tradeoff:** These guidelines bias toward caution over speed. For trivial tasks, use judgment.

## 1. Think Before Coding

**Don't assume. Don't hide confusion. Surface tradeoffs.**

Before implementing:
- State your assumptions explicitly. If uncertain, ask.
- If multiple interpretations exist, present them - don't pick silently.
- If a simpler approach exists, say so. Push back when warranted.
- If something is unclear, stop. Name what's confusing. Ask.

## 2. Simplicity First

**Minimum code that solves the problem. Nothing speculative.**

- No features beyond what was asked.
- No abstractions for single-use code.
- No "flexibility" or "configurability" that wasn't requested.
- No error handling for impossible scenarios.
- If you write 200 lines and it could be 50, rewrite it.

Ask yourself: "Would a senior engineer say this is overcomplicated?" If yes, simplify.

## 3. Surgical Changes

**Touch only what you must. Clean up only your own mess.**

When editing existing code:
- Don't "improve" adjacent code, comments, or formatting.
- Don't refactor things that aren't broken.
- Match existing style, even if you'd do it differently.
- If you notice unrelated dead code, mention it - don't delete it.

When your changes create orphans:
- Remove imports/variables/functions that YOUR changes made unused.
- Don't remove pre-existing dead code unless asked.

The test: Every changed line should trace directly to the user's request.

## 4. Goal-Driven Execution

**Define success criteria. Loop until verified.**

Transform tasks into verifiable goals:
- "Add validation" → "Write tests for invalid inputs, then make them pass"
- "Fix the bug" → "Write a test that reproduces it, then make it pass"
- "Refactor X" → "Ensure tests pass before and after"

For multi-step tasks, state a brief plan:
```
1. [Step] → verify: [check]
2. [Step] → verify: [check]
3. [Step] → verify: [check]
```

Strong success criteria let you loop independently. Weak criteria ("make it work") require constant clarification.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
PORT="${PORT:-6008}"
SERVER_PID=""
READINESS_URL="http://127.0.0.1:${PORT}/v1/models"

cleanup() {
    if [[ -n "$SERVER_PID" ]] && kill -0 "$SERVER_PID" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        kill "$SERVER_PID" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
        wait "$SERVER_PID" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    fi
}
trap cleanup EXIT
trap 'exit 130' INT TERM

if curl -fsS "$READINESS_URL" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "A vLLM server is already responding at $READINESS_URL." >&2
    echo "Stop it or set PORT to an unused port before precompiling." >&2
    exit 1
fi

echo "Precompiling Tongyi with the current Neuron vLLM configuration."
echo "The temporary server will stop after compilation and load validation succeed."

HOST=127.0.0.1 PORT="$PORT" bash "$SCRIPT_DIR/serve_tongyi.bash" &
SERVER_PID=$!

until curl -fsS "$READINESS_URL" >/dev/null 2>&1; do
    if ! kill -0 "$SERVER_PID" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        set +e
        wait "$SERVER_PID"
        status=$?
        set -e
        SERVER_PID=""
        if [[ "$status" -eq 0 ]]; then
            status=1
        fi
        echo "Tongyi precompilation failed before the server became ready." >&2
        exit "$status"
    fi
    sleep 10
done

echo "Compilation artifacts loaded successfully; stopping the temporary server."
cleanup
SERVER_PID=""

echo "Precompilation complete. Start the server with:"
echo "  bash bash/neuron/serve_tongyi.bash"
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

MODEL="${MODEL:?Set MODEL to the Hugging Face model id or local checkpoint path}"
REQUESTED_MODEL="$MODEL"
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
REPO_ROOT="$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)"
PYTHON_BIN="${PYTHON_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/.venv-neuron/bin/python}"
PORT="${PORT:-8000}"
TP_SIZE="${TP_SIZE:-4}"
MAX_MODEL_LEN="${MAX_MODEL_LEN:-4096}"
MAX_NUM_SEQS="${MAX_NUM_SEQS:-1}"
BLOCK_SIZE="${BLOCK_SIZE:-128}"
NUM_GPU_BLOCKS_OVERRIDE="${NUM_GPU_BLOCKS_OVERRIDE:-}"
ENABLE_PREFIX_CACHING="${ENABLE_PREFIX_CACHING:-}"
ENABLE_CHUNKED_PREFILL="${ENABLE_CHUNKED_PREFILL:-}"
HOST="${HOST:-0.0.0.0}"
NEURON_FRAMEWORK="${VLLM_NEURON_FRAMEWORK:-neuronx-distributed-inference}"
NEURON_CACHE_TAG="${NEURON_CACHE_TAG:-default}"

if [[ "$PYTHON_BIN" != /* && -e "$PYTHON_BIN" ]]; then
    PYTHON_BIN="$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "$PYTHON_BIN")" && pwd)/$(basename -- "$PYTHON_BIN")"
fi

SERVED_MODEL_NAME="${SERVED_MODEL_NAME:-$REQUESTED_MODEL}"
TOKENIZER="${TOKENIZER:-$REQUESTED_MODEL}"
LOCAL_MODEL_PATH="$REPO_ROOT/local-models/$REQUESTED_MODEL"
MODEL_SOURCE="hugging-face"

has_complete_local_weights() {
    local index_file

    [[ -f "$LOCAL_MODEL_PATH/config.json" ]] || return 1
    if [[ -f "$LOCAL_MODEL_PATH/model.safetensors" || -f "$LOCAL_MODEL_PATH/pytorch_model.bin" ]]; then
        return 0
    fi

    for index_file in model.safetensors.index.json pytorch_model.bin.index.json; do
        if [[ -f "$LOCAL_MODEL_PATH/$index_file" ]]; then
            "$PYTHON_BIN" - "$LOCAL_MODEL_PATH" "$index_file" <<'PY'
import json
import sys
from pathlib import Path

model_dir = Path(sys.argv[1])
index = json.loads((model_dir / sys.argv[2]).read_text())
shards = set(index.get("weight_map", {}).values())
raise SystemExit(not shards or any(not (model_dir / shard).is_file() for shard in shards))
PY
            return
        fi
    done

    return 1
}

if [[ -e "$REQUESTED_MODEL" ]]; then
    if [[ "$REQUESTED_MODEL" == /* ]]; then
        MODEL="$REQUESTED_MODEL"
    else
        MODEL="$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "$REQUESTED_MODEL")" && pwd)/$(basename -- "$REQUESTED_MODEL")"
    fi
    MODEL_SOURCE="local-explicit"
elif has_complete_local_weights; then
    MODEL="$LOCAL_MODEL_PATH"
    MODEL_SOURCE="local-cache"
fi

if [[ -z "${OVERRIDE_NEURON_CONFIG:-}" ]]; then
    OVERRIDE_NEURON_CONFIG=$(printf '{"enable_bucketing":true,"seq_len":%s,"max_context_length":%s}' \
        "$MAX_MODEL_LEN" "$MAX_MODEL_LEN")
fi

export VLLM_NEURON_FRAMEWORK="$NEURON_FRAMEWORK"
export PATH="$(dirname -- "$PYTHON_BIN"):$PATH"

if [[ -z "${NEURON_COMPILED_ARTIFACTS:-}" ]]; then
    stack_versions="$("$PYTHON_BIN" -c '
from importlib.metadata import PackageNotFoundError, version

packages = (
    "vllm",
    "vllm-neuron",
    "torch-neuronx",
    "neuronx-distributed",
    "neuronx-distributed-inference",
    "neuronx-cc",
    "nki",
)

def installed_version(name):
    try:
        return version(name)
    except PackageNotFoundError:
        return "missing"

print("|".join(f"{name}={installed_version(name)}" for name in packages))
')"
    cache_fingerprint="$(printf '%s\n' \
        "model=$REQUESTED_MODEL" \
        "tp_size=$TP_SIZE" \
        "max_model_len=$MAX_MODEL_LEN" \
        "max_num_seqs=$MAX_NUM_SEQS" \
        "block_size=$BLOCK_SIZE" \
        "num_gpu_blocks_override=$NUM_GPU_BLOCKS_OVERRIDE" \
        "enable_prefix_caching=$ENABLE_PREFIX_CACHING" \
        "enable_chunked_prefill=$ENABLE_CHUNKED_PREFILL" \
        "framework=$NEURON_FRAMEWORK" \
        "override_neuron_config=$OVERRIDE_NEURON_CONFIG" \
        "extra_vllm_args=${EXTRA_VLLM_ARGS:-}" \
        "cache_tag=$NEURON_CACHE_TAG" \
        "stack=$stack_versions")"
    cache_hash="$(sha256sum <<< "$cache_fingerprint")"
    cache_hash="${cache_hash%% *}"
    NEURON_COMPILED_ARTIFACTS="$REPO_ROOT/local-models/neuron-compiled-artifacts/$cache_hash"
fi
export NEURON_COMPILED_ARTIFACTS

artifact_state="cold"
if [[ -f "$NEURON_COMPILED_ARTIFACTS/model.pt" ]]; then
    artifact_state="ready"
elif [[ -n "$(find "$NEURON_COMPILED_ARTIFACTS" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -print -quit 2>/dev/null)" ]]; then
    artifact_state="incomplete"
fi

args=(
    "$PYTHON_BIN" -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server
    --model "$MODEL"
    --tokenizer "$TOKENIZER"
    --host "$HOST"
    --port "$PORT"
    --tensor-parallel-size "$TP_SIZE"
    --max-model-len "$MAX_MODEL_LEN"
    --max-num-seqs "$MAX_NUM_SEQS"
    --block-size "$BLOCK_SIZE"
)

args+=(--served-model-name "$SERVED_MODEL_NAME")

if [[ -n "$NUM_GPU_BLOCKS_OVERRIDE" ]]; then
    args+=(--num-gpu-blocks-override "$NUM_GPU_BLOCKS_OVERRIDE")
fi

case "$ENABLE_PREFIX_CACHING" in
    1 | true) args+=(--enable-prefix-caching) ;;
    0 | false) args+=(--no-enable-prefix-caching) ;;
    "") ;;
    *) echo "ENABLE_PREFIX_CACHING must be 0, 1, true, or false" >&2; exit 2 ;;
esac

case "$ENABLE_CHUNKED_PREFILL" in
    1 | true) args+=(--enable-chunked-prefill) ;;
    0 | false) args+=(--no-enable-chunked-prefill) ;;
    "") ;;
    *) echo "ENABLE_CHUNKED_PREFILL must be 0, 1, true, or false" >&2; exit 2 ;;
esac

if [[ -n "$OVERRIDE_NEURON_CONFIG" ]]; then
    args+=(--additional-config "{\"override_neuron_config\":$OVERRIDE_NEURON_CONFIG}")
fi

if [[ -n "${EXTRA_VLLM_ARGS:-}" ]]; then
    read -r -a extra_args <<< "$EXTRA_VLLM_ARGS"
    args+=("${extra_args[@]}")
fi

printf 'Starting Neuron vLLM server for %s on port %s (TP=%s, max_len=%s, max_seqs=%s, block_size=%s)\n' \
    "$SERVED_MODEL_NAME" "$PORT" "$TP_SIZE" "$MAX_MODEL_LEN" "$MAX_NUM_SEQS" "$BLOCK_SIZE"
printf 'Model weights: %s (%s)\n' "$MODEL" "$MODEL_SOURCE"
printf 'Tokenizer: %s\n' "$TOKENIZER"
printf 'Neuron compiled artifacts: %s (%s, tag=%s)\n' \
    "$NEURON_COMPILED_ARTIFACTS" "$artifact_state" "$NEURON_CACHE_TAG"
cd -- "$REPO_ROOT"
exec "${args[@]}"

tevatron = { git = "https://github.com/texttron/tevatron.git", rev = "main" }
# Repository Guidelines

## Project Structure & Module Organization
Core code is split between `search_agent/` and `searcher/`. Put model-facing clients, prompts, and shared agent utilities in `search_agent/`; keep retrieval servers and retriever implementations in `searcher/` and `searcher/searchers/`. Use `scripts_build_index/` for dataset/index setup and `scripts_evaluation/` for scoring outputs. Provider-specific run notes belong in `docs/`, benchmark inputs live in `topics-qrels/`, and figures belong in `figures/`. Treat `data/`, `indexes/`, `runs/`, `evals/`, `logs/`, and `cache/` as generated experiment artifacts unless a change explicitly needs tracked fixtures.

## Build, Test, and Development Commands
Target Python 3.10 and manage the environment with `uv`:

```bash
uv sync
source .venv/bin/activate
uv pip install --no-build-isolation flash-attn
```

Common workflows:

```bash
python scripts_build_index/decrypt_dataset.py --output data/browsecomp_plus_decrypted.jsonl --generate-tsv topics-qrels/queries.tsv
bash scripts_build_index/download_indexes.sh
python search_agent/openai_client.py --model o3 --output-dir runs/bm25/o3 --searcher-type bm25 --index-path indexes/bm25/ --num-threads 10
python scripts_evaluation/evaluate_run.py --input_dir runs/bm25/o3 --tensor_parallel_size 1
```

## Coding Style & Naming Conventions
Follow existing Python conventions: 4-space indentation, PEP 8 spacing, and `snake_case` for modules, files, functions, and CLI flags. Use `PascalCase` for classes. Keep new retrievers aligned with `searcher/searchers/base.py`, and expose configuration through explicit `argparse` options instead of hard-coded constants. Prefer small helper functions over long monolithic scripts.

## Testing Guidelines
There is no dedicated `tests/` directory or enforced coverage threshold yet. Validate changes with focused smoke runs against the affected entry point, then re-run `python scripts_evaluation/evaluate_run.py --input_dir ...` on a representative output directory. For retriever changes, verify both retrieval and document-fetch paths before opening a PR.

## Commit & Pull Request Guidelines
Recent history uses short, imperative subjects such as `add cache` and `build index`. Keep commit titles brief, lowercase, and specific to the change. PRs should describe the workflow affected, list required models, indexes, and API keys, link related issues, and include exact verification commands. If output changes are user-visible, attach example metrics or screenshots. Never commit `.env` files, API secrets, decrypted datasets, indexes, or run logs.

## Security & Configuration Tips
Several entry points load environment variables with `python-dotenv`, so keep local secrets in untracked `.env` files. Document any new required variables in the relevant `docs/*.md` file and provide safe defaults where possible.

## Working Style
- State assumptions explicitly when requirements are ambiguous; do not guess silently.
- Prefer the simplest solution that fully solves the requested problem.
- Make surgical changes only; avoid refactoring unrelated code or reformatting untouched files.
- Do not add speculative abstractions, configurability, or defensive code that is not needed for the task.
- When multiple approaches are viable, surface the tradeoffs before choosing.
- Define clear success criteria and verify with the most targeted command or test that fits the change.
- Take consider the multithreads and thread safety implications of changes, especially in retrievers and shared utilities.

## Verification & Smoke Tests

  When making code changes that affect runtime behavior, run the most targeted smoke test before finishing.

  GPU use:
  - Before GPU smoke tests, inspect available GPUs with `nvidia-smi` when available.
  - Prefer a single idle or least-used GPU by setting `CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=<gpu_id>`.
  - Do not assume GPU 0 is available.
  - If no GPU is available, state that the GPU smoke test could not be run and tell the user what to do to run it when a GPU is available.

  Smoke test scope:
  - Keep smoke tests small and targeted, for example one query, one batch, or `--max-queries 1`.
  - Do not run full benchmark/evaluation jobs unless explicitly requested.
  - Use temporary or clearly named smoke output paths such as:
    - `/tmp/<project>_codex_smoke_<timestamp>/`
    - `runs/smoke/<timestamp>/`
    - `cache/smoke/<timestamp>/`
    - `logs/smoke/<timestamp>/`

  Cleanup:
  - After a smoke test finishes, delete only the files/directories created by that exact smoke test.
  - Never delete existing `runs/`, `cache/`, `logs/`, `evals/`, `data/`, or `indexes/` directories broadly.
  - If cleanup fails, report the exact leftover path.
  - Prefer commands that make cleanup explicit, for example using a unique smoke directory and removing only that directory.

## Project Maintenance
- The baseline is tongyi_get and tongyi_filter_all. 
- The context-limit set is the questions that hit the context limit in tongyi_get, and the non-context-limit set is the questions that do not hit the context limit in tongyi_get. We use two separate exlicit sets to analyze the effect of duplicate documents in agentic search.

## Agent skills

### Issue tracker

Issues and PRDs are tracked with GitHub Issues. See `docs/agents/issue-tracker.md`.

### Triage labels

Use the five default canonical triage labels. See `docs/agents/triage-labels.md`.

### Domain docs

This is a single-context repository. See `docs/agents/domain.md`.
# BrowseComp-Plus

A benchmark for evaluating Deep-Research LLM agents against a fixed, curated corpus (~100K docs) instead of the live web, to isolate the retriever from the LLM agent for fair comparison.

## Language

**CL set / Context-limit set**:
Questions where the no-filter baseline (`tongyi_get`) hits the 108K token context limit during its trajectory, triggering a forced answer.
_Avoid_: OOM set, token-overflow set

**Non-CL set / Non-context-limit set**:
Questions where `tongyi_get` completes without hitting the context limit.
_Avoid_: normal set, non-limit set

**Same-right sub-set**:
Questions in the Non-CL set where both `tongyi_get` and a filter variant (e.g. `filter_all`) answer correctly.
_Avoid_: both-right, consistent-correct

**Regression sub-set**:
Questions in the Non-CL set where `tongyi_get` answers correctly but a filter variant answers incorrectly — the filtering causes a regression.
_Avoid_: degradation set, broke-set

**Improve sub-set**:
Questions in the Non-CL set where `tongyi_get` answers incorrectly but a filter variant answers correctly.
_Avoid_: fixed set, gain set

**Same-wrong sub-set**:
Questions in the Non-CL set where both `tongyi_get` and a filter variant answer incorrectly.
_Avoid_: both-wrong

**Checkpoint**:
A search_step that is a multiple of 4, where the branching test forks the trajectory into filter-on and filter-off sibling branches.
_Avoid_: fork point, split point

**Critical Checkpoint**:
A Checkpoint whose filter-on and filter-off descendant subtrees differ notably in outcome distributions such as correctness, completion, retrieval recall, or termination status. Automated scores are visual cues for human inspection, not definitive classifications.
_Avoid_: critical point

**Branch**:
A single trajectory variant with a specific filter-mode schedule (e.g. "rounds 1-3 no-filter, rounds 4-7 filter, rounds 8+ no-filter"). Branches terminate when the agent produces an answer, hits the context limit, or exhausts the 100-round budget.
_Avoid_: path, variant

**Leaf**:
A branch that has terminated. Its output is a standard `run_*.json` ready for evaluation.
_Avoid_: terminal node, endpoint

**Search step**:
The count of successful `search` tool calls in a trajectory. `get_document` calls and pure LLM reasoning rounds do not increment this counter.
_Avoid_: round, turn

**Duplicate document / Dup**:
A document returned by `search` whose docid has already been seen in a previous search call within the same trajectory.
_Avoid_: repeated doc, collision

**Filter mode**:
A binary state per branch (`filter_all=True` or `filter_all=False`) that controls whether the SearchToolHandler filters out previously-seen documents from search results.
_Avoid_: dedup mode, filter flag

**Marker mode**:
The style of annotation added when a Duplicate document is filtered from a search result, used to preserve duplicate-retrieval signal without repeating the full document text.
_Avoid_: marker flag, duplicate prompt

实现方案如下：

1. 新增一个很小的 Python 启动器，只替换 NxD `model_builder` 内的默认编译线程池；显式 `max_workers` 和 vLLM 其他线程池保持原样。
2. `compile_tongyi.bash` 默认 `NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS=2`，用 `flock` 防止重复预编译，并自动重启到 `MemoryHigh=92G / MemoryMax=104G` 的 user systemd scope。
3. 通用 Neuron launcher 仅在设置 worker 变量时走新启动器，普通 serve 路径不变。
4. 添加聚焦测试、文档和 shell/Python 烟测；不会启动真实编译。

const patch = "*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: /dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus/search_agent/neuron_vllm_launcher.py
+"""Launch vLLM with a bounded NxD HLO compilation thread pool."""
+
+import concurrent.futures
+import os
+import runpy
+
+
+DEFAULT_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS = 2
+
+
+def get_compile_max_workers() -> int:
+    raw_value = os.environ.get(
+        "NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS", str(DEFAULT_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS)
+    )
+    try:
+        max_workers = int(raw_value)
+    except ValueError as exc:
+        raise SystemExit("NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS must be a positive integer") from exc
+    if max_workers < 1:
+        raise SystemExit("NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS must be a positive integer")
+    return max_workers
+
+
+class _FuturesProxy:
+    def __init__(self, default_max_workers: int) -> None:
+        self.default_max_workers = default_max_workers
+
+    def ThreadPoolExecutor(self, max_workers=None, *args, **kwargs):
+        if max_workers is None:
+            max_workers = self.default_max_workers
+        return concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(
+            max_workers=max_workers, *args, **kwargs
+        )
+
+    def __getattr__(self, name):
+        return getattr(concurrent.futures, name)
+
+
+class _ConcurrentProxy:
+    def __init__(self, default_max_workers: int) -> None:
+        self.futures = _FuturesProxy(default_max_workers)
+
+
+def limit_nxd_compile_workers(max_workers: int) -> None:
+    from neuronx_distributed.trace import model_builder
+
+    # ModelBuilder imports the concurrent module and creates an executor without
+    # a limit. Replace only its module reference so other vLLM pools are untouched.
+    model_builder.concurrent = _ConcurrentProxy(max_workers)
+
+
+def main() -> None:
+    max_workers = get_compile_max_workers()
+    limit_nxd_compile_workers(max_workers)
+    print(f"NxD HLO compile workers: {max_workers}", flush=True)
+    runpy.run_module("vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server", run_name="__main__")
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    main()
*** Add File: /dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus/tests/test_neuron_vllm_launcher.py
+import concurrent.futures
+
+import pytest
+
+from search_agent import neuron_vllm_launcher
+
+
+def test_get_compile_max_workers_defaults_to_two(monkeypatch):
+    monkeypatch.delenv("NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS", raising=False)
+
+    assert neuron_vllm_launcher.get_compile_max_workers() == 2
+
+
+@pytest.mark.parametrize("value", ["0", "-1", "invalid"])
+def test_get_compile_max_workers_rejects_invalid_values(monkeypatch, value):
+    monkeypatch.setenv("NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS", value)
+
+    with pytest.raises(SystemExit, match="must be a positive integer"):
+        neuron_vllm_launcher.get_compile_max_workers()
+
+
+def test_futures_proxy_limits_only_implicit_worker_count():
+    proxy = neuron_vllm_launcher._FuturesProxy(default_max_workers=2)
+
+    with proxy.ThreadPoolExecutor() as executor:
+        assert executor._max_workers == 2
+    with proxy.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=4) as executor:
+        assert executor._max_workers == 4
+    assert proxy.as_completed is concurrent.futures.as_completed
*** Update File: /dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus/bash/neuron/serve_vllm_neuron.bash
@@
 args=(
-    "$PYTHON_BIN" -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server
+    "$PYTHON_BIN" -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server
@@
 )
+
+if [[ -n "${NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS:-}" ]]; then
+    args=(
+        "$PYTHON_BIN" -m search_agent.neuron_vllm_launcher
+        "${args[@]:3}"

*** Update File: /dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus/bash/neuron/compile_tongyi.bash
@@
 SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
+REPO_ROOT="$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)"
 PORT="${PORT:-6008}"
+NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS="${NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS:-2}"
+NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_HIGH="${NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_HIGH:-92G}"
+NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_MAX="${NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_MAX:-104G}"
+NEURON_COMPILE_USE_SYSTEMD_SCOPE="${NEURON_COMPILE_USE_SYSTEMD_SCOPE:-1}"
 SERVER_PID=""
 READINESS_URL="http://127.0.0.1:${PORT}/v1/models"
+
+if [[ ! "$NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS" =~ ^[1-9][0-9]*$ ]]; then
+    echo "NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS must be a positive integer." >&2
+    exit 2
+fi
+
+if [[ "$NEURON_COMPILE_USE_SYSTEMD_SCOPE" == "1" && "${NEURON_COMPILE_SCOPE_ACTIVE:-0}" != "1" ]]; then
+    if ! command -v systemd-run >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+        echo "systemd-run is required for the Neuron compile memory limit." >&2
+        echo "Set NEURON_COMPILE_USE_SYSTEMD_SCOPE=0 only if another cgroup limit is active." >&2
+        exit 1
+    fi
+    exec systemd-run --user --scope --quiet \
+        -p "MemoryHigh=$NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_HIGH" \
+        -p "MemoryMax=$NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_MAX" \
+        -p MemorySwapMax=0 \
+        env \
+        NEURON_COMPILE_SCOPE_ACTIVE=1 \
+        NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS="$NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS" \
+        NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_HIGH="$NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_HIGH" \
+        NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_MAX="$NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_MAX" \
+        bash "$0" "$@"
+fi
+
+mkdir -p "$REPO_ROOT/local-models/neuron-compiled-artifacts"
+exec 9>"$REPO_ROOT/local-models/neuron-compiled-artifacts/.tongyi-compile.lock"
+if ! flock -n 9; then
+    echo "Another Tongyi precompilation is already running." >&2
+    exit 1
+fi
@@
 echo "Precompiling Tongyi with the current Neuron vLLM configuration."
+echo "NxD HLO compile workers: $NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS"
+if [[ "$NEURON_COMPILE_USE_SYSTEMD_SCOPE" == "1" ]]; then
+    echo "Compile memory limits: high=$NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_HIGH max=$NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_MAX"
+fi
 echo "The temporary server will stop after compilation and load validation succeed."
 
-HOST=127.0.0.1 PORT="$PORT" bash "$SCRIPT_DIR/serve_tongyi.bash" &
+HOST=127.0.0.1 \
+PORT="$PORT" \
+NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS="$NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS" \
+    bash "$SCRIPT_DIR/serve_tongyi.bash" &
*** Update File: /dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus/docs/neuron_trn2.md
@@
 - `NEURON_COMPILED_ARTIFACTS`: advanced explicit compiled-artifact directory
   override.
+- `NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS`: maximum concurrent NxD HLO compiler jobs. The
+  Tongyi precompile wrapper defaults to `2`; direct serving leaves NxD unchanged
+  unless this variable is set.
+- `NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_HIGH` and `NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_MAX`: soft and hard
+  cgroup limits used by `compile_tongyi.bash`. They default to `92G` and `104G`
+  for the 124 GiB Trn2 host.
+- `NEURON_COMPILE_USE_SYSTEMD_SCOPE`: set to `0` only when the compile already
+  runs under an equivalent cgroup limit. It defaults to `1`.
 - `PYTHON_BIN`: Python executable from the Neuron environment.
@@
 The first launch still performs weight conversion and Neuron compilation.
 Later launches with exactly the same model and compilation settings reuse the
 compiled artifacts. Changing a compilation setting creates a separate cache
 directory instead of reusing an incompatible model.
+
+Precompile Tongyi with timestamped application and kernel logs:
+
+```bash
+LOG_DIR="logs/neuron-debug/$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ)"
+mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR"
+nohup journalctl -kf -o short-iso > "$LOG_DIR/kernel.log" 2>&1 &
+set -o pipefail
+NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS=2 bash bash/neuron/compile_tongyi.bash \
+  2>&1 | tee "$LOG_DIR/compile.log"
+```
+
+The wrapper serializes Tongyi precompiles with `flock` and runs the complete
+compiler process tree in a user systemd scope. The hard memory limit terminates
+the compile rather than allowing concurrent `neuronx-cc` processes to make the
+instance unresponsive. Increasing the worker count does not change the compiled
+artifact cache key; test `4` only after a complete run with `2` stays below the
+memory limit.
*** End Patch";
text(await tools.apply_patch(patch));

 - `NEURON_COMPILED_ARTIFACTS`: advanced explicit compiled-artifact directory
   override.
+- `NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS`: maximum concurrent NxD HLO compiler jobs. The
+  Tongyi precompile wrapper defaults to `2`; direct serving leaves NxD unchanged
+  unless this variable is set.
+- `NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_HIGH` and `NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_MAX`: soft and hard
+  cgroup limits used by `compile_tongyi.bash`. They default to `92G` and `104G`
+  for the 124 GiB Trn2 host.
+- `NEURON_COMPILE_USE_SYSTEMD_SCOPE`: set to `0` only when the compile already
+  runs under an equivalent cgroup limit. It defaults to `1`.
 - `PYTHON_BIN`: Python executable from the Neuron environment.
@@
 The first launch still performs weight conversion and Neuron compilation.
 Later launches with exactly the same model and compilation settings reuse the
 compiled artifacts. Changing a compilation setting creates a separate cache
 directory instead of reusing an incompatible model.
+
+Precompile Tongyi with timestamped application and kernel logs:
+
+```bash
+LOG_DIR="logs/neuron-debug/$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ)"
+mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR"
+nohup journalctl -kf -o short-iso > "$LOG_DIR/kernel.log" 2>&1 &
+set -o pipefail
+NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS=2 bash bash/neuron/compile_tongyi.bash \
+  2>&1 | tee "$LOG_DIR/compile.log"
+```
+
+The wrapper serializes Tongyi precompiles with `flock` and runs the complete
+compiler process tree in a user systemd scope. The hard memory limit terminates
+the compile rather than allowing concurrent `neuronx-cc` processes to make the
+instance unresponsive. Increasing the worker count does not change the compiled
+artifact cache key; test `4` only after a complete run with `2` stays below the
+memory limit.

*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: search_agent/neuron_vllm_launcher.py
+"""Launch vLLM with a bounded NxD HLO compilation thread pool."""
+
+import concurrent.futures
+import os
+import runpy
+
+
+DEFAULT_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS = 2
+
+
+def get_compile_max_workers() -> int:
+    raw_value = os.environ.get(
+        "NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS", str(DEFAULT_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS)
+    )
+    try:
+        max_workers = int(raw_value)
+    except ValueError as exc:
+        raise SystemExit("NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS must be a positive integer") from exc
+    if max_workers < 1:
+        raise SystemExit("NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS must be a positive integer")
+    return max_workers
+
+
+class _FuturesProxy:
+    def __init__(self, default_max_workers: int) -> None:
+        self.default_max_workers = default_max_workers
+
+    def ThreadPoolExecutor(self, max_workers=None, *args, **kwargs):
+        if max_workers is None:
+            max_workers = self.default_max_workers
+        return concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(
+            max_workers=max_workers, *args, **kwargs
+        )
+
+    def __getattr__(self, name):
+        return getattr(concurrent.futures, name)
+
+
+class _ConcurrentProxy:
+    def __init__(self, default_max_workers: int) -> None:
+        self.futures = _FuturesProxy(default_max_workers)
+
+
+def limit_nxd_compile_workers(max_workers: int) -> None:
+    from neuronx_distributed.trace import model_builder
+
+    # ModelBuilder creates an unbounded-by-workload executor. Replace only its
+    # module reference so other vLLM thread pools keep their original defaults.
+    model_builder.concurrent = _ConcurrentProxy(max_workers)
+
+
+def main() -> None:
+    max_workers = get_compile_max_workers()
+    limit_nxd_compile_workers(max_workers)
+    print(f"NxD HLO compile workers: {max_workers}", flush=True)
+    runpy.run_module("vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server", run_name="__main__")
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    main()

+    bash "$SCRIPT_DIR/serve_tongyi.bash" &
*** Update File: docs/neuron_trn2.md
@@
 - `NEURON_COMPILED_ARTIFACTS`: advanced explicit compiled-artifact directory
   override.
+- `NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS`: maximum concurrent NxD HLO compiler jobs. The
+  Tongyi precompile wrapper defaults to `2`; direct serving leaves NxD unchanged
+  unless this variable is set.
+- `NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_HIGH` and `NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_MAX`: soft and hard
+  cgroup limits used by `compile_tongyi.bash`. They default to `92G` and `104G`
+  for the 124 GiB Trn2 host.
+- `NEURON_COMPILE_USE_SYSTEMD_SCOPE`: set to `0` only when the compile already
+  runs under an equivalent cgroup limit. It defaults to `1`.
 - `PYTHON_BIN`: Python executable from the Neuron environment.
@@
 The first launch still performs weight conversion and Neuron compilation.
 Later launches with exactly the same model and compilation settings reuse the
 compiled artifacts. Changing a compilation setting creates a separate cache
 directory instead of reusing an incompatible model.
+
+Precompile Tongyi with timestamped application and kernel logs:
+
+```bash
+LOG_DIR="logs/neuron-debug/$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ)"
+mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR"
+nohup journalctl -kf -o short-iso > "$LOG_DIR/kernel.log" 2>&1 &
+set -o pipefail
+NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS=2 bash bash/neuron/compile_tongyi.bash \
+  2>&1 | tee "$LOG_DIR/compile.log"
+```
+
+The wrapper serializes Tongyi precompiles with `flock` and runs the complete
+compiler process tree in a user systemd scope. The hard memory limit terminates
+the compile rather than allowing concurrent `neuronx-cc` processes to make the
+instance unresponsive. Increasing the worker count does not change the compiled
+artifact cache key; test `4` only after a complete run with `2` stays below the
+memory limit.
*** End Patch";

payload.input:
const patch = "*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: search_agent/neuron_vllm_launcher.py
+"""Launch vLLM with a bounded NxD HLO compilation thread pool."""
+
+import concurrent.futures
+import os
+import runpy
+
+
+DEFAULT_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS = 2
+
+
+def get_compile_max_workers() -> int:
+    raw_value = os.environ.get(
+        "NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS", str(DEFAULT_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS)
+    )
+    try:
+        max_workers = int(raw_value)
+    except ValueError as exc:
+        raise SystemExit("NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS must be a positive integer") from exc
+    if max_workers < 1:
+        raise SystemExit("NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS must be a positive integer")
+    return max_workers
+
+
+class _FuturesProxy:
+    def __init__(self, default_max_workers: int) -> None:
+        self.default_max_workers = default_max_workers
+
+    def ThreadPoolExecutor(self, max_workers=None, *args, **kwargs):
+        if max_workers is None:
+            max_workers = self.default_max_workers
+        return concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(
+            max_workers=max_workers, *args, **kwargs
+        )
+
+    def __getattr__(self, name):
+        return getattr(concurrent.futures, name)
+
+
+class _ConcurrentProxy:
+    def __init__(self, default_max_workers: int) -> None:
+        self.futures = _FuturesProxy(default_max_workers)
+
+
+def limit_nxd_compile_workers(max_workers: int) -> None:
+    from neuronx_distributed.trace import model_builder
+
+    # ModelBuilder creates an unbounded-by-workload executor. Replace only its
+    # module reference so other vLLM thread pools keep their original defaults.
+    model_builder.concurrent = _ConcurrentProxy(max_workers)
+
+
+def main() -> None:
+    max_workers = get_compile_max_workers()
+    limit_nxd_compile_workers(max_workers)
+    print(f"NxD HLO compile workers: {max_workers}", flush=True)
+    runpy.run_module("vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server", run_name="__main__")
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    main()
*** Add File: tests/test_neuron_vllm_launcher.py
+import concurrent.futures
+
+import pytest
+
+from search_agent import neuron_vllm_launcher
+
+
+def test_get_compile_max_workers_defaults_to_two(monkeypatch):
+    monkeypatch.delenv("NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS", raising=False)
+
+    assert neuron_vllm_launcher.get_compile_max_workers() == 2
+
+
+@pytest.mark.parametrize("value", ["0", "-1", "invalid"])
+def test_get_compile_max_workers_rejects_invalid_values(monkeypatch, value):
+    monkeypatch.setenv("NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS", value)
+
+    with pytest.raises(SystemExit, match="must be a positive integer"):
+        neuron_vllm_launcher.get_compile_max_workers()
+
+
+def test_futures_proxy_limits_only_implicit_worker_count():
+    proxy = neuron_vllm_launcher._FuturesProxy(default_max_workers=2)
+
+    with proxy.ThreadPoolExecutor() as executor:
+        assert executor._max_workers == 2
+    with proxy.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=4) as executor:
+        assert executor._max_workers == 4
+    assert proxy.as_completed is concurrent.futures.as_completed
*** Update File: bash/neuron/serve_vllm_neuron.bash
@@

     "$PYTHON_BIN" -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server
@@

+

@@
 SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
+REPO_ROOT="$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)"
 PORT="${PORT:-6008}"
+NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS="${NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS:-2}"
+NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_HIGH="${NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_HIGH:-92G}"
+NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_MAX="${NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_MAX:-104G}"
+NEURON_COMPILE_USE_SYSTEMD_SCOPE="${NEURON_COMPILE_USE_SYSTEMD_SCOPE:-1}"
 SERVER_PID=""
 READINESS_URL="http://127.0.0.1:${PORT}/v1/models"
+
+if [[ ! "$NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS" =~ ^[1-9][0-9]*$ ]]; then
+    echo "NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS must be a positive integer." >&2
+    exit 2
+fi
+
+if [[ "$NEURON_COMPILE_USE_SYSTEMD_SCOPE" == "1" && "${NEURON_COMPILE_SCOPE_ACTIVE:-0}" != "1" ]]; then
+    if ! command -v systemd-run >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+        echo "systemd-run is required for the Neuron compile memory limit." >&2
+        echo "Set NEURON_COMPILE_USE_SYSTEMD_SCOPE=0 only if another cgroup limit is active." >&2
+        exit 1
+    fi
+    exec systemd-run --user --scope --quiet \
+        -p "MemoryHigh=$NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_HIGH" \
+        -p "MemoryMax=$NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_MAX" \
+        -p MemorySwapMax=0 \
+        env \
+        NEURON_COMPILE_SCOPE_ACTIVE=1 \
+        NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS="$NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS" \
+        NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_HIGH="$NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_HIGH" \
+        NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_MAX="$NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_MAX" \
+        bash "$0" "$@"
+fi
+
+mkdir -p "$REPO_ROOT/local-models/neuron-compiled-artifacts"
+exec 9>"$REPO_ROOT/local-models/neuron-compiled-artifacts/.tongyi-compile.lock"
+if ! flock -n 9; then
+    echo "Another Tongyi precompilation is already running." >&2
+    exit 1
+fi
@@

@@

payload.message: 验证暴露了两个环境问题，不是实现逻辑问题：仓库两个 venv 都没有 `pytest`，所以测试会改成标准库 `unittest`；Neuron 启动器的直接烟测没有继承 `serve_vllm_neuron.bash` 会设置的 `.venv-neuron/bin` PATH，因此找不到 `libneuronpjrt-path`。我会修正测试依赖并按真实 launcher 环境重跑。

content.text: 验证暴露了两个环境问题，不是实现逻辑问题：仓库两个 venv 都没有 `pytest`，所以测试会改成标准库 `unittest`；Neuron 启动器的直接烟测没有继承 `serve_vllm_neuron.bash` 会设置的 `.venv-neuron/bin` PATH，因此找不到 `libneuronpjrt-path`。我会修正测试依赖并按真实 launcher 环境重跑。

const patch = "*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: tests/test_neuron_vllm_launcher.py
@@
 import concurrent.futures
+import os
+import unittest
+from unittest import mock
 
-import pytest
-
 from search_agent import neuron_vllm_launcher
 
 
-def test_get_compile_max_workers_defaults_to_two(monkeypatch):
-    monkeypatch.delenv("NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS", raising=False)
+class NeuronVllmLauncherTest(unittest.TestCase):
+    @mock.patch.dict(os.environ, {}, clear=True)
+    def test_get_compile_max_workers_defaults_to_two(self):
+        self.assertEqual(neuron_vllm_launcher.get_compile_max_workers(), 2)
 
-    assert neuron_vllm_launcher.get_compile_max_workers() == 2
+    def test_get_compile_max_workers_rejects_invalid_values(self):
+        for value in ("0", "-1", "invalid"):
+            with self.subTest(value=value):
+                with mock.patch.dict(
+                    os.environ, {"NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS": value}, clear=True
+                ):
+                    with self.assertRaisesRegex(
+                        SystemExit, "must be a positive integer"
+                    ):
+                        neuron_vllm_launcher.get_compile_max_workers()
 
+    def test_futures_proxy_limits_only_implicit_worker_count(self):
+        proxy = neuron_vllm_launcher._FuturesProxy(default_max_workers=2)
 
-@pytest.mark.parametrize("value", ["0", "-1", "invalid"])
-def test_get_compile_max_workers_rejects_invalid_values(monkeypatch, value):
-    monkeypatch.setenv("NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS", value)
+        with proxy.ThreadPoolExecutor() as executor:
+            self.assertEqual(executor._max_workers, 2)
+        with proxy.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=4) as executor:
+            self.assertEqual(executor._max_workers, 4)
+        self.assertIs(proxy.as_completed, concurrent.futures.as_completed)
 
-    with pytest.raises(SystemExit, match="must be a positive integer"):
-        neuron_vllm_launcher.get_compile_max_workers()
 
-
-def test_futures_proxy_limits_only_implicit_worker_count():
-    proxy = neuron_vllm_launcher._FuturesProxy(default_max_workers=2)
-
-    with proxy.ThreadPoolExecutor() as executor:
-        assert executor._max_workers == 2
-    with proxy.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=4) as executor:
-        assert executor._max_workers == 4
-    assert proxy.as_completed is concurrent.futures.as_completed
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    unittest.main()
*** End Patch";
if (patch.includes("'")) { throw new Error("Patch contains single quote"); }
const r = await tools.exec_command({"cmd":"apply_patch '" + patch + "'","workdir":"/dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus","yield_time_ms":10000,"max_output_tokens":8000,"sandbox_permissions":"require_escalated","justification":"May I replace the pytest-only focused test with a standard-library unittest so it runs in the existing repository environment?","prefix_rule":["apply_patch"]});
text(r.output);

Config Groups:
  positional arguments    
  options                 
  Frontend                Arguments for the OpenAI-compatible frontend server.
  ModelConfig             Configuration for the model.

  AttentionConfig         Configuration for attention mechanisms in vLLM.
  StructuredOutputsConfig Dataclass which contains structured outputs config for the engine.
  ParallelConfig          Configuration for the distributed execution.
  CacheConfig             Configuration for the KV cache.
  MultiModalConfig        Controls the behavior of multimodal models.
  LoRAConfig              Configuration for LoRA.
  ObservabilityConfig     Configuration for observability - metrics and tracing.
  SchedulerConfig         Scheduler configuration.
  CompilationConfig       Configuration for compilation.
  
      You must pass CompilationConfig to VLLMConfig constructor.
      VLLMConfig's post_init does further initialization. If used outside of the
      VLLMConfig, some fields will be left in an improper state.
  
      It has three parts:
  
      - Top-level Compilation control:
          - [`mode`][vllm.config.CompilationConfig.mode]
          - [`debug_dump_path`][vllm.config.CompilationConfig.debug_dump_path]
          - [`cache_dir`][vllm.config.CompilationConfig.cache_dir]

          - [`splitting_ops`][vllm.config.CompilationConfig.splitting_ops]
          - [`compile_mm_encoder`][vllm.config.CompilationConfig.compile_mm_encoder]
      - CudaGraph capture:
          - [`cudagraph_mode`][vllm.config.CompilationConfig.cudagraph_mode]
          - [`cudagraph_capture_sizes`]
          [vllm.config.CompilationConfig.cudagraph_capture_sizes]
          - [`max_cudagraph_capture_size`]
          [vllm.config.CompilationConfig.max_cudagraph_capture_size]
          - [`cudagraph_num_of_warmups`]
          [vllm.config.CompilationConfig.cudagraph_num_of_warmups]
          - [`cudagraph_copy_inputs`]
          [vllm.config.CompilationConfig.cudagraph_copy_inputs]
      - Inductor compilation:
          - [`compile_sizes`][vllm.config.CompilationConfig.compile_sizes]
          - [`compile_ranges_split_points`]
              [vllm.config.CompilationConfig.compile_ranges_split_points]
          - [`inductor_compile_config`]
          [vllm.config.CompilationConfig.inductor_compile_config]
          - [`inductor_passes`][vllm.config.CompilationConfig.inductor_passes]
          - custom inductor passes
  
      Why we have different sizes for cudagraph and inductor:
      - cudagraph: a cudagraph captured for a specific size can only be used
          for the same size. We need to capture all the sizes we want to use.
      - inductor: a graph compiled by inductor for a general shape can be used
          for different sizes. Inductor can also compile for specific sizes,
          where it can have more information to optimize the graph with fully
          static shapes. However, we find the general shape compilation is
          sufficient for most cases. It might be beneficial to compile for
          certain small batchsizes, where inductor is good at optimizing.
      
  KernelConfig            Configuration for kernel selection and warmup behavior.
  VllmConfig              Dataclass which contains all vllm-related configuration. This
      simplifies passing around the distinct configurations in the codebase.

"""Launch vLLM with a bounded NxD HLO compilation thread pool."""

import concurrent.futures
import os
import runpy


DEFAULT_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS = 2


def get_compile_max_workers() -> int:
    raw_value = os.environ.get(
        "NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS", str(DEFAULT_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS)
    )
    try:
        max_workers = int(raw_value)
    except ValueError as exc:
        raise SystemExit("NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS must be a positive integer") from exc
    if max_workers < 1:
        raise SystemExit("NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS must be a positive integer")
    return max_workers


class _FuturesProxy:
    def __init__(self, default_max_workers: int) -> None:
        self.default_max_workers = default_max_workers

    def ThreadPoolExecutor(self, max_workers=None, *args, **kwargs):
        if max_workers is None:
            max_workers = self.default_max_workers
        return concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(
            max_workers=max_workers, *args, **kwargs
        )

    def __getattr__(self, name):
        return getattr(concurrent.futures, name)


class _ConcurrentProxy:
    def __init__(self, default_max_workers: int) -> None:
        self.futures = _FuturesProxy(default_max_workers)


def limit_nxd_compile_workers(max_workers: int) -> None:
    from neuronx_distributed.trace import model_builder

    # ModelBuilder creates an unbounded-by-workload executor. Replace only its
    # module reference so other vLLM thread pools keep their original defaults.
    model_builder.concurrent = _ConcurrentProxy(max_workers)


def main() -> None:
    max_workers = get_compile_max_workers()
    limit_nxd_compile_workers(max_workers)
    print(f"NxD HLO compile workers: {max_workers}", flush=True)
    runpy.run_module("vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server", run_name="__main__")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
import concurrent.futures
import os
import unittest
from unittest import mock

from search_agent import neuron_vllm_launcher


class NeuronVllmLauncherTest(unittest.TestCase):
    @mock.patch.dict(os.environ, {}, clear=True)
    def test_get_compile_max_workers_defaults_to_two(self):
        self.assertEqual(neuron_vllm_launcher.get_compile_max_workers(), 2)

    def test_get_compile_max_workers_rejects_invalid_values(self):
        for value in ("0", "-1", "invalid"):
            with self.subTest(value=value):
                with mock.patch.dict(
                    os.environ, {"NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS": value}, clear=True
                ):
                    with self.assertRaisesRegex(
                        SystemExit, "must be a positive integer"
                    ):
                        neuron_vllm_launcher.get_compile_max_workers()

    def test_futures_proxy_limits_only_implicit_worker_count(self):
        proxy = neuron_vllm_launcher._FuturesProxy(default_max_workers=2)

        with proxy.ThreadPoolExecutor() as executor:
            self.assertEqual(executor._max_workers, 2)
        with proxy.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=4) as executor:
            self.assertEqual(executor._max_workers, 4)
        self.assertIs(proxy.as_completed, concurrent.futures.as_completed)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    unittest.main()

RESULT 1
---
name: karpathy-guidelines
description: Behavioral guidelines to reduce common LLM coding mistakes. Use when writing, reviewing, or refactoring code to avoid overcomplication, make surgical changes, surface assumptions, and define verifiable success criteria.
license: MIT
---

# Karpathy Guidelines

Behavioral guidelines to reduce common LLM coding mistakes, derived from [Andrej Karpathy's observations](https://x.com/karpathy/status/2015883857489522876) on LLM coding pitfalls.

**Tradeoff:** These guidelines bias toward caution over speed. For trivial tasks, use judgment.

## 1. Think Before Coding

**Don't assume. Don't hide confusion. Surface tradeoffs.**

Before implementing:
- State your assumptions explicitly. If uncertain, ask.
- If multiple interpretations exist, present them - don't pick silently.
- If a simpler approach exists, say so. Push back when warranted.
- If something is unclear, stop. Name what's confusing. Ask.

## 2. Simplicity First

**Minimum code that solves the problem. Nothing speculative.**

- No features beyond what was asked.
- No abstractions for single-use code.
- No "flexibility" or "configurability" that wasn't requested.
- No error handling for impossible scenarios.
- If you write 200 lines and it could be 50, rewrite it.

Ask yourself: "Would a senior engineer say this is overcomplicated?" If yes, simplify.

## 3. Surgical Changes

**Touch only what you must. Clean up only your own mess.**

When editing existing code:
- Don't "improve" adjacent code, comments, or formatting.
- Don't refactor things that aren't broken.
- Match existing style, even if you'd do it differently.
- If you notice unrelated dead code, mention it - don't delete it.

When your changes create orphans:
- Remove imports/variables/functions that YOUR changes made unused.
- Don't remove pre-existing dead code unless asked.

The test: Every changed line should trace directly to the user's request.

## 4. Goal-Driven Execution

**Define success criteria. Loop until verified.**

Transform tasks into verifiable goals:
- "Add validation" → "Write tests for invalid inputs, then make them pass"
- "Fix the bug" → "Write a test that reproduces it, then make it pass"
- "Refactor X" → "Ensure tests pass before and after"

For multi-step tasks, state a brief plan:
```
1. [Step] → verify: [check]
2. [Step] → verify: [check]
3. [Step] → verify: [check]
```

Strong success criteria let you loop independently. Weak criteria ("make it work") require constant clarification.

RESULT 2
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
REPO_ROOT="$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)"
PORT="${PORT:-6008}"
NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS="${NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS:-2}"
NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_HIGH="${NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_HIGH:-92G}"
NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_MAX="${NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_MAX:-104G}"
NEURON_COMPILE_USE_SYSTEMD_SCOPE="${NEURON_COMPILE_USE_SYSTEMD_SCOPE:-1}"
SERVER_PID=""
READINESS_URL="http://127.0.0.1:${PORT}/v1/models"

if [[ ! "$NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS" =~ ^[1-9][0-9]*$ ]]; then
    echo "NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS must be a positive integer." >&2
    exit 2
fi

if [[ "$NEURON_COMPILE_USE_SYSTEMD_SCOPE" == "1" && "${NEURON_COMPILE_SCOPE_ACTIVE:-0}" != "1" ]]; then
    if ! command -v systemd-run >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "systemd-run is required for the Neuron compile memory limit." >&2
        echo "Set NEURON_COMPILE_USE_SYSTEMD_SCOPE=0 only if another cgroup limit is active." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
    exec systemd-run --user --scope --quiet \
        -p "MemoryHigh=$NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_HIGH" \
        -p "MemoryMax=$NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_MAX" \
        -p MemorySwapMax=0 \
        env \
        NEURON_COMPILE_SCOPE_ACTIVE=1 \
        NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS="$NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS" \
        NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_HIGH="$NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_HIGH" \
        NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_MAX="$NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_MAX" \
        bash "$0" "$@"
fi

mkdir -p "$REPO_ROOT/local-models/neuron-compiled-artifacts"
exec 9>"$REPO_ROOT/local-models/neuron-compiled-artifacts/.tongyi-compile.lock"
if ! flock -n 9; then
    echo "Another Tongyi precompilation is already running." >&2
    exit 1
fi

cleanup() {
    if [[ -n "$SERVER_PID" ]] && kill -0 "$SERVER_PID" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        kill "$SERVER_PID" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
        wait "$SERVER_PID" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    fi
}
trap cleanup EXIT
trap 'exit 130' INT TERM

if curl -fsS "$READINESS_URL" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "A vLLM server is already responding at $READINESS_URL." >&2
    echo "Stop it or set PORT to an unused port before precompiling." >&2
    exit 1
fi

echo "Precompiling Tongyi with the current Neuron vLLM configuration."
echo "NxD HLO compile workers: $NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS"
if [[ "$NEURON_COMPILE_USE_SYSTEMD_SCOPE" == "1" ]]; then
    echo "Compile memory limits: high=$NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_HIGH max=$NEURON_COMPILE_MEMORY_MAX"
fi
echo "The temporary server will stop after compilation and load validation succeed."

HOST=127.0.0.1 \
PORT="$PORT" \
NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS="$NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS" \
    bash "$SCRIPT_DIR/serve_tongyi.bash" &
SERVER_PID=$!

until curl -fsS "$READINESS_URL" >/dev/null 2>&1; do
    if ! kill -0 "$SERVER_PID" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        set +e
        wait "$SERVER_PID"
        status=$?
        set -e
        SERVER_PID=""
        if [[ "$status" -eq 0 ]]; then
            status=1
        fi
        echo "Tongyi precompilation failed before the server became ready." >&2
        exit "$status"
    fi
    sleep 10
done

echo "Compilation artifacts loaded successfully; stopping the temporary server."
cleanup
SERVER_PID=""

echo "Precompilation complete. Start the server with:"
echo "  bash bash/neuron/serve_tongyi.bash"
  model directory contains weights but not tokenizer files.
- Traced and compiled Neuron models are stored under
  `local-models/neuron-compiled-artifacts/<configuration-hash>/`. The hash
  includes the model id, tensor parallel size, sequence length, concurrency,
  block size, Neuron overrides, extra vLLM arguments, cache tag, and relevant
  package versions.

The first launch still performs weight conversion and Neuron compilation.
Later launches with exactly the same model and compilation settings reuse the
compiled artifacts. Changing a compilation setting creates a separate cache
directory instead of reusing an incompatible model.

Precompile Tongyi with timestamped application and kernel logs:

```bash
LOG_DIR="logs/neuron-debug/$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ)"
mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR"
nohup journalctl -kf -o short-iso > "$LOG_DIR/kernel.log" 2>&1 &
set -o pipefail
NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS=2 bash bash/neuron/compile_tongyi.bash \
  2>&1 | tee "$LOG_DIR/compile.log"
```

The wrapper serializes Tongyi precompiles with `flock` and runs the complete
compiler process tree in a user systemd scope. The hard memory limit terminates
the compile rather than allowing concurrent `neuronx-cc` processes to make the
instance unresponsive. Increasing the worker count does not change the compiled
artifact cache key; test `4` only after a complete run with `2` stays below the
memory limit.

The startup log reports a compiled cache as `cold` when the directory is empty,
`incomplete` when an interrupted compile left partial files, and `ready` only
when NxD's complete `model.pt` artifact exists. NxD still performs its own load
validation before serving requests.

Set `NEURON_CACHE_TAG` when a model revision or local compiler-related change
is not represented by the normal launch arguments:

```bash
NEURON_CACHE_TAG=tongyi-revision-2 bash bash/neuron/serve_tongyi.bash
```

`NEURON_COMPILED_ARTIFACTS` remains an advanced explicit override. Use it only
for one model and one fixed configuration; vLLM Neuron loads a valid artifact
from that path even when the current launch arguments differ. Do not point
Tongyi and the Qwen judge at the same explicit directory.

## Smoke test a server

```bash
curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:8032/v1/models
 M bash/neuron/compile_tongyi.bash
 M bash/neuron/serve_vllm_neuron.bash
 M docs/neuron_trn2.md
?? search_agent/neuron_vllm_launcher.py
?? tests/test_neuron_vllm_launcher.py

 - `NEURON_CACHE_TAG`: manual compiled-cache invalidation tag.

const r = await tools.exec_command({"cmd":"set -euo pipefail
smoke_root="/tmp/browsecomp_neuron_log_smoke_$$"
mkdir -p "$smoke_root/v1"
touch "$smoke_root/v1/models"
python3 -m http.server 6099 --bind 127.0.0.1 --directory "$smoke_root" > "$smoke_root/server.log" 2>&1 &
server_pid=$!
log_dir=""
cleanup_smoke() {
    kill "$server_pid" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    wait "$server_pid" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    if [[ -n "$log_dir" && "$log_dir" == "$PWD/logs/neuron-debug/"* ]]; then
        rm -rf "$log_dir"
    fi
    rm -rf "$smoke_root"
}
trap cleanup_smoke EXIT
for _ in 1 2 3 4 5; do
    if curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:6099/v1/models >/dev/null; then
        break
    fi
    sleep 1
done
set +e
PORT=6099 bash bash/neuron/compile_tongyi.bash > "$smoke_root/output.log" 2>&1
status=$?
set -e
cat "$smoke_root/output.log"
log_dir="$(sed -n 's/^Neuron compile logs: //p' "$smoke_root/output.log" | tail -n 1)"
[[ "$status" -eq 1 ]]
[[ -n "$log_dir" ]]
[[ -s "$log_dir/compile.log" ]]
[[ -f "$log_dir/kernel.log" ]]
[[ -s "$log_dir/compile.pid" ]]
[[ -s "$log_dir/kernel.pid" ]]
kernel_pid="$(<"$log_dir/kernel.pid")"
! kill -0 "$kernel_pid" >/dev/null 2>&1
printf 'Self-logging smoke test passed: %s\n' "$log_dir"","workdir":"/dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus","yield_time_ms":30000,"max_output_tokens":12000,"sandbox_permissions":"require_escalated","justification":"May I run a safe self-logging smoke test against a local fake readiness endpoint, then delete only the temporary server and exact generated smoke log directory?","prefix_rule":["bash","bash/neuron/compile_tongyi.bash"]});
text(r.output);

payload.message:
已内置自动日志记录。现在只需在 `tmux` 中运行：

```bash
bash bash/neuron/compile_tongyi.bash
```

默认使用 2 个编译 worker。测试 4 个时：

```bash
NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS=4 bash bash/neuron/compile_tongyi.bash
```

[compile_tongyi.bash](/dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus/bash/neuron/compile_tongyi.bash:40) 会自动：

- 创建 `logs/neuron-debug/<UTC时间>/`
- 同时输出到终端和 `compile.log`
- 持续记录内核消息到 `kernel.log`
- 写入 `compile.pid` 和 `kernel.pid`
- 自动使用 92G/104G cgroup 内存限制
- 退出时停止内核日志进程
- 用 `flock` 阻止重复编译

脚本启动时会打印实际日志目录。另一个终端也可查看最新日志：

```bash
LOG_DIR="$(ls -dt logs/neuron-debug/* | head -n 1)"
tail -f "$LOG_DIR/compile.log"
```

验证完成：3 个单元测试、shell 语法、systemd scope 和自动日志烟测均通过；烟测文件及进程已清理，未启动真实 Neuron 编译。

content.text: 我会使用 `handoff` skill，把本次 Trainium 故障诊断、已修改代码、缓存清理状态和下一步运行/监控命令压缩成可直接续接的交接摘要。

## Handoff Summary

### Current State

Diagnosed repeated Trainium instance hangs during:

```bash
bash bash/neuron/compile_tongyi.bash
```

Root cause evidence:

- NxD uses `concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor()` without `max_workers`.
- Host has 12 CPUs, so Python defaults to 16 workers.
- Multiple 128K HLO compilations ran concurrently on a 124GiB RAM host with no swap.
- Kernel logged repeated `systemd-journald: Under memory pressure` before SSH and EC2 status checks stopped responding.
- Four compiler-cache modules contained failed NEFF records from prior `ENOSPC`/SIGHUP failures.
- AWS System log only showed the forced reboot, EXT4 journal recovery, and a post-boot Neuron reset timeout. It did not contain the pre-crash period.

### Implemented Changes

Modified:

- `bash/neuron/compile_tongyi.bash`
- `bash/neuron/serve_vllm_neuron.bash`
- `docs/neuron_trn2.md`

Added:

- `search_agent/neuron_vllm_launcher.py`
- `tests/test_neuron_vllm_launcher.py`

Behavior now:

- Default `NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS=2`; user may override with `4`.
- Only NxD ModelBuilder’s implicit HLO thread pool is limited; other vLLM thread pools are untouched.
- Compile runs in a user systemd scope:
  - `MemoryHigh=92G`
  - `MemoryMax=104G`
  - `MemorySwapMax=0`
- `flock` prevents simultaneous Tongyi precompiles.
- Script automatically creates:

```text
logs/neuron-debug/<UTC timestamp>/
  compile.log
  kernel.log
  compile.pid
  kernel.pid
```

- Output is shown in the terminal and written to `compile.log`.
- `journalctl -kf` is cleaned up when the script exits.

### Cache Cleanup

Moved four failed modules out of active lookup into:

```text
/var/tmp/neuron-failed-cache-20260712T125500Z/
```

Modules:

```text
MODULE_49f6f8ab8ab5e527253a+7d9f298c
MODULE_818a43d73b7c0c8dbf3e+31fd8a0e
MODULE_83a329f01e816d47edb2+d6b08f8e
MODULE_abbc48cb2b1a814fa105+af768c68
```

Moved the incomplete artifact to:

```text
local-models/neuron-compiled-artifacts/6367e6277bb96bbebd100738dae73a788bc99baac16bdf380edc9489556230d2.failed-20260712T125500Z
```

Successful compiler-cache entries and the 32GB NKI intermediate cache were preserved.

### Verification

Passed:

- `bash -n` for both modified shell scripts.
- `git diff --check`.
- Three standard-library unit tests.
- vLLM launcher `--help` smoke test; it printed `NxD HLO compile workers: 2`.
- User systemd scope property smoke test.
- Self-logging smoke test against a fake local `/v1/models`; no Neuron compilation started.
- Smoke artifacts/processes were cleaned up.
- No active failed compiler-cache records were found afterward.

Full 128K compilation has not been rerun.

### Current Git State

Expected uncommitted changes:

```text
 M bash/neuron/compile_tongyi.bash
 M bash/neuron/serve_vllm_neuron.bash
 M docs/neuron_trn2.md
?? search_agent/neuron_vllm_launcher.py
?? tests/test_neuron_vllm_launcher.py
```

Do not revert these changes.

### User Preferences and Constraints

- User wants one-command operation with automatic logs.
- Default workers should be 2, not 1.
- Four workers may be tested later, but the 104G memory cap must remain.
- Preserve useful compiler caches; quarantine specific failed entries instead of deleting the entire cache.
- Do not launch full benchmark jobs or destructive cleanup.
- The host’s Codex `bwrap` sandbox often fails with:

```text
bwrap: loopback: Failed RTM_NEWADDR: Operation not permitted
```

Read/write commands may require approved escalated execution. Continue using `apply_patch`; when its sandbox helper fails, the successful fallback was host-side `apply_patch` with carefully shell-quoted patch text.

### Next Steps

Run from `tmux`:

```bash
bash bash/neuron/compile_tongyi.bash
```

For an experimental four-worker run:

```bash
NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS=4 bash bash/neuron/compile_tongyi.bash
```

Monitor latest log:

```bash
LOG_DIR="$(ls -dt logs/neuron-debug/* | head -n 1)"
tail -f "$LOG_DIR/compile.log"
```

During the real run, watch memory and compiler processes:

```bash
watch -n 3 'free -h; ps -eo pid,%cpu,%mem,rss,etime,args | grep -E "neuronx-cc|EngineCore"'
```

Success requires:

```text
Compilation artifacts loaded successfully; stopping the temporary server.
Precompilation complete.
```

If the cgroup kills the run, inspect `compile.log`, `kernel.log`, and cgroup memory events before changing workers or memory limits. Do not raise `MemoryMax`; reduce workers from 2 to 1 if necessary.

Added:

- `search_agent/neuron_vllm_launcher.py`
- `tests/test_neuron_vllm_launcher.py`

Behavior now:

- Default `NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS=2`; user may override with `4`.
- Only NxD ModelBuilder’s implicit HLO thread pool is limited; other vLLM thread pools are untouched.
- Compile runs in a user systemd scope:
  - `MemoryHigh=92G`
  - `MemoryMax=104G`
  - `MemorySwapMax=0`
- `flock` prevents simultaneous Tongyi precompiles.
- Script automatically creates:

```text
logs/neuron-debug/<UTC timestamp>/
  compile.log
  kernel.log
  compile.pid
  kernel.pid
```

- Output is shown in the terminal and written to `compile.log`.
- `journalctl -kf` is cleaned up when the script exits.

### Cache Cleanup

Moved four failed modules out of active lookup into:

```text
/var/tmp/neuron-failed-cache-20260712T125500Z/
```

Modules:

```text
MODULE_49f6f8ab8ab5e527253a+7d9f298c
MODULE_818a43d73b7c0c8dbf3e+31fd8a0e
MODULE_83a329f01e816d47edb2+d6b08f8e
MODULE_abbc48cb2b1a814fa105+af768c68
```

Moved the incomplete artifact to:

```text
local-models/neuron-compiled-artifacts/6367e6277bb96bbebd100738dae73a788bc99baac16bdf380edc9489556230d2.failed-20260712T125500Z
```

Successful compiler-cache entries and the 32GB NKI intermediate cache were preserved.

### Verification

Passed:

- `bash -n` for both modified shell scripts.
- `git diff --check`.
- Three standard-library unit tests.
- vLLM launcher `--help` smoke test; it printed `NxD HLO compile workers: 2`.
- User systemd scope property smoke test.
- Self-logging smoke test against a fake local `/v1/models`; no Neuron compilation started.
- Smoke artifacts/processes were cleaned up.
- No active failed compiler-cache records were found afterward.

Full 128K compilation has not been rerun.

### Current Git State

Expected uncommitted changes:

```text
 M bash/neuron/compile_tongyi.bash
 M bash/neuron/serve_vllm_neuron.bash
 M docs/neuron_trn2.md
?? search_agent/neuron_vllm_launcher.py
?? tests/test_neuron_vllm_launcher.py
```

Do not revert these changes.

### User Preferences and Constraints

- User wants one-command operation with automatic logs.
- Default workers should be 2, not 1.
- Four workers may be tested later, but the 104G memory cap must remain.
- Preserve useful compiler caches; quarantine specific failed entries instead of deleting the entire cache.
- Do not launch full benchmark jobs or destructive cleanup.
- The host’s Codex `bwrap` sandbox often fails with:

```text
bwrap: loopback: Failed RTM_NEWADDR: Operation not permitted
```

Read/write commands may require approved escalated execution. Continue using `apply_patch`; when its sandbox helper fails, the successful fallback was host-side `apply_patch` with carefully shell-quoted patch text.

### Next Steps

Run from `tmux`:

```bash
bash bash/neuron/compile_tongyi.bash
```

For an experimental four-worker run:

```bash
NXD_COMPILE_MAX_WORKERS=4 bash bash/neuron/compile_tongyi.bash
```

Monitor latest log:

```bash
LOG_DIR="$(ls -dt logs/neuron-debug/* | head -n 1)"
tail -f "$LOG_DIR/compile.log"
```

During the real run, watch memory and compiler processes:

```bash
watch -n 3 'free -h; ps -eo pid,%cpu,%mem,rss,etime,args | grep -E "neuronx-cc|EngineCore"'
```

Success requires:

```text
Compilation artifacts loaded successfully; stopping the temporary server.
Precompilation complete.
```

If the cgroup kills the run, inspect `compile.log`, `kernel.log`, and cgroup memory events before changing workers or memory limits. Do not raise `MemoryMax`; reduce workers from 2 to 1 if necessary.

# Escalation Requests

Commands are run outside the sandbox if they are approved by the user, or match an existing rule that allows it to run unrestricted. The command string is split into independent command segments at shell control operators, including but not limited to:

- Pipes: |
- Logical operators: &&, ||
- Command separators: ;
- Subshell boundaries: (...), $(...)

Each resulting segment is evaluated independently for sandbox restrictions and approval requirements.

Example:

git pull | tee output.txt

This is treated as two command segments:

["git", "pull"]

["tee", "output.txt"]

Commands that use more advanced shell features like redirection (>, >>, <), substitutions ($(...), ...), environment variables (FOO=bar), or wildcard patterns (*, ?) will not be evaluated against rules, to limit the scope of what an approved rule allows.

- hugging-face:hf-cli: Hugging Face Hub CLI (`hf`) for downloading, uploading, and managing repositories, models, datasets, and Spaces on the Hugging Face Hub. Replaces now deprecated `huggingface-cli` command. (file: r4/cli/SKILL.md)
- hugging-face:huggingface-community-evals: Run evaluations for Hugging Face Hub models using inspect-ai and lighteval on local hardware. Use for backend selection, local GPU evals, and choosing between vLLM / Transformers / accelerate. Not for HF Jobs orchestration, model-card PRs, .eval_results publication, or community-evals automation. (file: r4/community-evals/SKILL.md)
- hugging-face:huggingface-datasets: Use this skill for Hugging Face Dataset Viewer API workflows that fetch subset/split metadata, paginate rows, search text, apply filters, download parquet URLs, and read size or statistics. (file: r4/datasets/SKILL.md)
- hugging-face:huggingface-gradio: Build Gradio web UIs and demos in Python. Use when creating or editing Gradio apps, components, event listeners, layouts, or chatbots. (file: r4/gradio/SKILL.md)
- hugging-face:huggingface-jobs: This skill should be used when users want to run any workload on Hugging Face Jobs infrastructure. Covers UV scripts, Docker-based jobs, hardware selection, cost estimation, authentication with tokens, secrets management, timeout configuration, and result persistence. Designed for general-purpose compute workloads including data processing, inference, experiments, batch jobs, and any Python-based tasks. Should be invoked for tasks involving cloud compute, GPU workloads, or when users mention running jobs on Hugging Face infrastructure without local setup. (file: r4/jobs/SKILL.md)
- hugging-face:huggingface-llm-trainer: This skill should be used when users want to train or fine-tune language models using TRL (Transformer Reinforcement Learning) on Hugging Face Jobs infrastructure. Covers SFT, DPO, GRPO and reward modeling training methods, plus GGUF conversion for local deployment. Includes guidance on the TRL Jobs package, UV scripts with PEP 723 format, dataset preparation and validation, hardware selection, cost estimation, Trackio monitoring, Hub authentication, and model persistence. Should be invoked for tasks involving cloud GPU training, GGUF conversion, or when users mention training on Hugging Face Jobs without local GPU setup. (file: r4/llm-trainer/SKILL.md)
- hugging-face:huggingface-paper-publisher: Publish and manage research papers on Hugging Face Hub. Supports creating paper pages, linking papers to models/datasets, claiming authorship, and generating professional markdown-based research articles. (file: r4/paper-publisher/SKILL.md)
- hugging-face:huggingface-papers: Look up and read Hugging Face paper pages in markdown, and use the papers API for structured metadata such as authors, linked models/datasets/spaces, Github repo and project page. Use when the user shares a Hugging Face paper page URL, an arXiv URL or ID, or asks to summarize, explain, or analyze an AI research paper. (file: r4/papers/SKILL.md)
- hugging-face:huggingface-trackio: Track and visualize ML training experiments with Trackio. Use when logging metrics during training (Python API), firing alerts for training diagnostics, or retrieving/analyzing logged metrics (CLI). Supports real-time dashboard visualization, alerts with webhooks, HF Space syncing, and JSON output for automation. (file: r4/trackio/SKILL.md)
- hugging-face:huggingface-vision-trainer: Trains and fine-tunes vision models for object detection (D-FINE, RT-DETR v2, DETR, YOLOS), image classification (timm models — MobileNetV3, MobileViT, ResNet, ViT/DINOv3 — plus any Transformers classifier), and SAM/SAM2 segmentation using Hugging Face Transformers on Hugging Face Jobs cloud GPUs. Covers COCO-format dataset preparation, Albumentations augmentation, mAP/mAR evaluation, accuracy metrics, SAM segmentation with bbox/point prompts, DiceCE loss, hardware selection, cost estimation, Trackio monitoring, and Hub persistence. Use when users mention training object detection, image classification, SAM, SAM2, segmentation, image matting, DETR, D-FINE, RT-DETR, ViT, timm, MobileNet, ResNet, bounding box models, or fine-tuning vision models on Hugging Face Jobs. (file: r4/vision-trainer/SKILL.md)
- hugging-face:transformers-js: Use Transformers.js to run state-of-the-art machine learning models directly in JavaScript/TypeScript. Supports NLP (text classification, translation, summarization), computer vision (image classification, object detection), audio (speech recognition, audio classification), and multimodal tasks. Works in Node.js and browsers (with WebGPU/WASM) using pre-trained models from Hugging Face Hub. (file: r4/transformers.js/SKILL.md)
- hyperframes:gsap: GSAP animation reference for HyperFrames. Covers gsap.to(), from(), fromTo(), easing, stagger, defaults, timelines (gsap.timeline(), position parameter, labels, nesting, playback), and performance (transforms, will-change, quickTo). Use when writing GSAP animations in HyperFrames compositions. (file: r5/gsap/SKILL.md)
- hyperframes:hyperframes: Create video compositions, animations, title cards, overlays, captions, voiceovers, audio-reactive visuals, and scene transitions in HyperFrames HTML. Use when asked to build any HTML-based video content, add captions or subtitles synced to audio, generate text-to-speech narration, create audio-reactive animation (beat sync, glow, pulse driven by music), add animated text highlighting (marker sweeps, hand-drawn circles, burst lines, scribble, sketchout), or add transitions between scenes (crossfades, wipes, reveals, shader transitions). Covers composition authoring, timing, media, and the full video production workflow. For CLI commands (init, lint, preview, render, transcribe, tts) see the hyperframes-cli skill. (file: r5/hyperframes/SKILL.md)
- hyperframes:hyperframes-cli: HyperFrames CLI tool — hyperframes init, lint, inspect, preview, render, transcribe, tts, doctor, browser, info, upgrade, compositions, docs, benchmark. (file: r5/hyperframes-cli/SKILL.md)
- hyperframes:hyperframes-registry: Install and wire registry blocks and components into HyperFrames compositions. - hyperframes:website-to-hyperframes: Capture a website and create a HyperFrames video from it. (file: r0/json-translator/SKILL.md)
